channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump 2.0 has a Cabinet and executive branch of different positions and eclectic personalities
item.description: Donald Trump’s personnel choices for his new Cabinet and White House reflect his signature positions on immigration and trade but also a range of viewpoints and backgrounds that raise questions about what ideological anchors might guide his Oval Office encore.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The rising price of paying the national debt is a risk for Trump’s promises on growth and inflation
item.description: Donald Trump has big plans for the economy. He also has big debt problem that’ll be a hurdle to delivering on those plan. Trump has bold ambitions on tax cuts, tariffs and other programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: After Trump's win, Black women are rethinking their role as America's reliable political organizers
item.description: Donald Trump's victory has dismayed many politically engaged Black women, and they're reassessing their enthusiasm for politics and organizing.
item.dc:creator: Kenya Hunter, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Auchincloss: Senate must reject ‘dangerous’ RFK Jr. as HHS head
item.description: Auchincloss’s remarks about RFK echo the concerns of other lawmakers and Kennedy family members alike.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Brooke Rollins, Trump's pick for agriculture secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former White House aide Brooke Rollins to lead the Department of Agriculture in his second administration. Here are some things to know about Trump’s choice and the agency that Rollins would lead if she is confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Trump's Project 2025 denials, he is tapping its authors and influencers for key roles
item.description: During the campaign, President-elect Donald Trump had hailed what would become Project 2025 as a conservative roadmap for “exactly what our movement will do.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House mostly mum after Trump victory
item.description: The White House has taken its show largely behind closed doors after Vice President Harris’s stinging defeat to Donald Trump. Harris has all but disappeared from the public eye, jetting off to Hawaii after a few scarce appearances around Washington following her massive loss on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Republican Party is increasingly winning union voters. It's a shift seen in his labor pick
item.description: Working-class voters helped Republicans make steady election gains this year and expanded a coalition that increasingly includes rank-and-file union members.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump nominates former Texas lawmaker Scott Turner to lead Housing and Urban Development
item.description: Turner served in the Texas House for four years and was also executive director of the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii And Renzo Downey, KPRC 2

item.title: NJ Rep. Gottheimer vows to fight congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) — Rep. Josh Gottheimer has been a staunch opponent of congestion pricing since its introduction. Now, with a new plan set in motion by New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to tap hedge fund manager Scott Bessent to serve as Treasury secretary in his new administration, a source confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump encounters Senate GOP limits with failed Gaetz AG push
item.description: President-elect Trump's ill-fated nomination of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to run the Justice Department marked an early sign of his potential limits in moving his nominees and dealing with Senate Republicans, giving him less wiggle room to get his Cabinet in place.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican who lost Minnesota House race sues claiming DFL winner lives outside district
item.description: The Roseville-area court challenge is the latest legal action Republicans are using to try and break the tie in the Minnesota House next year and muster up a majority in one chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Senate reaches deal on stalled Biden judicial nominees
item.description: The Senate announced a deal Thursday to complete work on seven of President Biden’s nine stalled judicial nominees, who sparked an intense fight on the Senate floor this week.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Florida officials allege 'pervasive' discrimination against Trump supporters at FEMA
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody and U.S. Rep. Vern Buchanan will hold a news conference Friday "to discuss their efforts to uncover evidence of political discrimination at FEMA and demand accountability," according to a release from Buchanan's office.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump names former Florida AG Pam Bondi as his new pick for U.S. attorney general
item.description: After former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration, President-elect Donald Trump named Pam Bondi, a former attorney general of Florida, as his next pick for U.S. attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: Matt Gaetz has withdrawn as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general following scrutiny over a federal sex trafficking investigation. The Florida Republican made the announcement Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 14-vote lead for DFL state House candidate up for review in Scott County recount
item.description: County officials are hand-counting ballots in the contest that could determine control of the Minnesota House of Representatives.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
